title: Armor HUD
description: This mod puts armor in view next to the offhand slot, allowing you to always be able to see your durability.
body:
# Armor HUD
This mod allows you to see your equipped armor in an offhand type slot known as a HUD widget. 
This allows you to see the durability and change or repair it before it's too late. This mod also shows a warning before it breaks.

This mod adds a minimalistic vanilla-like widget that shows your armor items and a warning if durability is low enough.
While other mods expect the player to look at numbers all over the screen, this mod allows to see your armor items
in a vanilla-style minimalistic widget without extra text or numbers. Position and other parameters of the widget
can be adjusted in a [configuration file](./config.md).

## Additional Information
This mod is made for [Fabric Loader](https://fabricmc.net/use/ "Fabric").*
If [Fabric API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/fabric-api "Fabric API") is installed,
one can reload the config same way as resource packs.
If [Mod Menu](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/modmenu "Mod Menu")
and [Cloth Config API](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/cloth-config "Cloth Config API") is installed,
then one can configure this mod using a config screen accessible through mods menu.

## Contact
Website linked [here.](https://sites.google.com/view/armor-hud/)

Bug report and git repo on the site.

## NEOFORGE SUPPORT
*CubingXT has added Neoforge support to this mod in version 1.2.0 with assistance from AnAwesomGuy.

Neoforge support has been beta tested and passed.

## Credits
Thank you to BerdinskiyBear for originally making this mod.

Thank you to uku for forking BerdinskiyBear's Armor HUD to update past 1.19.

Thank you to AnAwesomGuy for developing the Neoforge code that has since been merged with mine.

I forked this mod originally to update to 1.21.8 back in 2025 but have kept this port running because some modpacks have included 
this mod in them, causing potential updates to the game breaking the modpack completely. I myself make modpacks and have gone through 
the pain of disabling each mod one by one until the game loads. <sub>And sometimes the game doesn't even load because you disabled an 
API required for another mod later down the line but have already disabled the originally problematic mod but because a critical API 
was disabled the game still crashes and you have to repeat the whole painful process all over again while simultaniously questioning 
your life choices and failing to come to terms with all of your hardships and failures and mistakes and the cycle continues on and on 
and on until you finally find the problematic mod and the game loads but then you need to find another mod to do the same thing and 
test it and it's time consuming but then you realize that the new update to the game is so new that there are no good mods that are 
updated and you're left feeling helpless.</sub> Anyways, I also keep this fork running because uku's Armor HUD is no longer on curseforge 
and I want to give modpacks a good BerdinskiyBear fork to run.
